rulings.Case Title: NELLI M. GRINBERG VS. CITY & COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, DEPRTMENT OF et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Motion To Vacate
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, November 13, 2012, Line 1, PLAINTIFF NELLI GRINBERG'S Motion To Vacate. Denied. Plaintiff Grinberg is not entitled to mandatory relief from summary judgment per Code of Civil Procedure § 473(b). (English v. IKON Business Solutions, Inc. (2001) 94 Cal.App.4th 130, 138.) Further, Ms. Grinberg is not entitled to discretionary relief. Plaintiff's counsel did not bring this motion in a reasonable time after April 11, 2012, which is when she discovered her asserted mistake in failing to calendar the motion for summary judgment, file an opposition, or attend the hearing which took place on April 4, 2012. (Gelber Decl., 4.) This motion was filed on October 10, 2012. Further, between the time she discovered her mistake, allegedly upon receiving the notice of entry of order on April 11, 2012, defense counsel sent plaintiff's counsel a letter dated April 16, 2012 with a proposed judgment, to which plaintiff did not respond. Defense counsel then served a notice of entry of judgment on June 8, 2012. Still Plaintiff's counsel did not respond. Plaintiff does not address or attempt to excuse her failures to respond to the proposed judgment or to take any steps after receiving the notice of entry on June 8, 2012. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: 560 HAIGHT STREET HOMEOWNERS ASSOCIATION INC A VS. 560 HAIGHT STREET, LLC A CALIFORNIA LIMITED
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiff'S Second Demand For Inspection And Production Of Documents
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, November 13, 2012, at 9:00 am, line 1, PLAINTIFF 560 HAIGHT STREET HOMEOWNERS ASSOCIATION INC'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiff's Second Demand For Inspection And Production Of Documents Pro Tem Judge James Watson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Grant the motion. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to jamesw@skwsf.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/JPT

rulings.Case Title: FRED GORSIRA VS. QUAKE COURIER AND ATTORNEY SERVICE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Strike
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, November 13, 2012, Line 13, PLAINTIFF FRED GORSIRA'S Motion To Strike. Granted. The fourth cause of action for abuse of process falls within the ambit of the anti-SLAPP statute. Cross-Complainants has not met their burden of showing a probability of success on the merits of any aspect of the abuse of process cause of action. Defendants/Cross-Complainant's Objections to Evidence are sustained. Attorney fees are awarded to Plaintiff/Cross-defendant Gorsira in an amount to be later determined upon a showing by Plaintiff/Cross-defendant. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: AMIR MORTAZAVI VS. BETTY NGUYEN
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Motion For Leave To Amend Cross Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, November 13, 2012, Line 15, DEFENDANT BETTY NGUYEN'S Motion For Leave To Amend Cross Complaint. The motion to amend the cross-complaint is granted. The proposed amendments do not change the tenor of the case and there is not substantial evidence that the amendments are being raised in bad faith. Department 206 will determine whether a trial continuance is appropriate. The Court denies Plaintiff's request for conditions. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: STEPHANIE WILLIS VS. TENDERLOIN NEIGHBORHOOD DEVELOPMENT CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, November 13, 2012, Line 19, DEFENDANT TENDERLOIN NEIGHBORHOOD DEVELOPMENT CORPORATION'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. The Court sustains the demurrer to the fifth cause of action for intentional infliction of emotional distress without leave to amend as conceded by Plaintiff. The Court sustains the demurrers to the remaining causes of action with 20 days leave to amend. Plaintiff has leave to amend that she and her fiancée did not share a supervisor/subordinate relationship and that the fiancée was allowed to remain working with defendant despite defendant's knowledge of the relationship. These allegations will support plaintiff's assertion that she had a reasonable expectation of privacy and that there was disparate treatment. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: VINCENZO CALOIARO et al VS. SCOTT SIDWELL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Scott Sidwell And University Of San Francisco'S Demurrer To The 1st Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, November 13, 2012, Line 30, Defendants Scott Sidwell And University Of San Francisco'S Demurrer To The 1st Amended Complaint. Sustained, with 20 days leave to amend as to all causes of action pertaining to Joan Caloiaro. CCP § 430.10(e). No cause of action contains any allegations of injury as to Joan Caloiaro, even taking into consideration the incorporation of certain paragraphs by reference. Further, to the extent the causes of action can be understood, the "injury" in these causes of action refers only to Vincent Caloiaro, and/or uses the singular "plaintiff" and/or masculine pronouns. Overruled regarding causes of action 4, 5, 6, 7, and 8 alleged against Scott Sidwell. Defendant relies on inapposite authority and has not shown as a matter of law that Defendant Sidwell is not liable. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/MJM)
